text.title: chunking-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for chunking-basics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Start with chunk_size equals 500 tokens and overlap equals 50 tokens. Use 1000-1500 tokens for technical/legal. Use 200-400 tokens for QA content.
rule.rationale: 500 tokens is a practical middle ground that preserves context while keeping chunks focused. Adjust based on retrieval benchmarks - no theoretical size works universally.

rule.statement: Always measure chunk token counts with tiktoken before indexing. Word counts diverge significantly from token counts.
rule.rationale: Token counts directly impact embedding model input budgets and retrieval quality. Word counts are approximately 30 percent inaccurate for token budgets.

rule.statement: Set overlap to 10-20 percent of chunk_size. For QA corpora where questions span chunk boundaries use 20 percent.
rule.rationale: Overlap prevents abrupt context loss at chunk boundaries. Queries that span boundaries benefit from overlapping chunks.

rule.statement: Attach source, page, chunk_index, and strategy metadata at creation time. Retrofitting metadata later requires full re-index.
rule.rationale: Metadata attached to chunks at creation enables source tracking, deduplication, and quality auditing downstream.

rule.statement: Use content-based IDs like md5(source_path + chunk_index) for chunk IDs, not sequential integers.
rule.rationale: Sequential IDs cause collisions when documents are updated or re-indexed. Content-based IDs enable idempotent re-ingestion.

rule.statement: Test chunking with at least five representative queries before committing to a strategy. Retrieval accuracy is the only ground truth.
rule.rationale: Theoretical chunking decisions rarely survive contact with real query patterns. Even 30 percent change in chunk size can dramatically affect retrieval.

rule.statement: For mixed corpora (documentation, code, chat), route each file type through its own chunker before merging into one index.
rule.rationale: A chunking strategy tuned for prose performs poorly on code and vice versa. Separate routing ensures each content type gets appropriate treatment.

rule.statement: Pre-clean text (strip headers/footers, normalize whitespace) before chunking. Noise propagates into all downstream chunks.
rule.rationale: Document headers, page numbers, and boilerplate become part of every chunk. Pre-cleaning prevents embedding model confusion.

rule.statement: Version the index name with the chunking config hash when strategy changes. Existing vector index entries become stale with new chunk sizes.
rule.rationale: Re-indexing with different chunk sizes invalidates relevance scores from the old index. Version tracking prevents accidental searches against mixed indices.
